mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month additions to nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth has been positive each month since October 2010
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines month-over-month but S&P 500 still posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of such instances over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 8.0%, it confirms the labor market recovery is entrenched, reducing recession fears and supporting consumer spending, which lifts equities over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE peaked at 10.0 in October 2009 and has been declining
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses below 8.0 the S&P 500 forward 60-day return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive monthly growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer and investment demand, which together signal a self-reinforcing expansion that drives equity markets higher over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS post month-over-month increases
evidence_refs: fact:Both series showed positive prints in recent months
failure_condition: If in months when both proxies increase the S&P 500 20-day return is negative more than 30% of the time over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month consecutive decline in industrial production (INDPRO) often precedes a policy response or a rebalancing of inventories that leads to a recovery, so the market prices a mean reversion and equities rise over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO declined modestly in mid-2012 but the economy remained in expansion
failure_condition: If after any 3-month INDPRO decline the S&P 500 20-day return is negative more than 50% of the time over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer spending momentum, which accounts for a large portion of GDP; a rising trend in RSAFS is a persistent positive force for corporate revenues and stock prices over short 5-day windows.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales have been generally increasing in 2012
failure_condition: If the weekly correlation between RSAFS releases and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is non-positive over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) above a threshold level (13600 billion chained dollars) confirms the aggregate economy is operating well above its recession trough, which boosts confidence and corporate profit outlook, leading to positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2012 GDP was 13558, below current threshold, but Q1 was 13472
failure_condition: If when GDPC1 exceeds 13600 the S&P 500 60-day return is negative more than 40% of the time over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) provides the strongest possible labor market signal, indicating both job creation and slack absorption; this joint improvement directly raises household incomes and consumption, driving equities higher over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines and PAYEMS increases in the latest month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has steadily fallen while PAYEMS has risen since late 2010
failure_condition: If in months where both conditions hold the S&P 500 20-day return is lower than the unconditional average return over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline consumer inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 230 index points, it implies subdued cost pressures, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy without tightening prematurely; this benign inflation environment supports equity valuations and risk-taking over 5-day horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI has been trending around 228-230 in 2012, well below peaks
failure_condition: If when CPIAUCSL is below 230 the S&P 500 5-day return has a Sharpe ratio less than 0 (negative risk-adjusted return) over any 2-year period.
